nativeRange.habitat: Shallow coral reefs and lagoons, typically at depths of 1–15 m, in association with host sea anemones

diet.notes: Generally accepts a wide variety of foods including high-quality marine flakes and pellets (ideally with spirulina), frozen mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and other frozen meaty foods. Has been documented feeding on zooplankton, algae, and small invertebrates in the wild.

imageCandidates.notes: Photographer: Ritiks; 3884×2600 px, hi-res; shows Amphiprion ocellaris in natural anemone habitat (Andaman Coral Reef). This is the standard orange morph, not the black morph — reviewer should confirm acceptability or prefer a black-morph image.

Photographer: J.E. Randall; FishBase species photo for Amphiprion ocellaris from Indonesia. Standard orange morph — not the black Darwin morph. Reviewer should verify URL resolves and confirm morph representation.

LiveAquaria product page for the Black Percula Clownfish tank-raised variant. Reviewer should extract the product hero image URL directly from the page; the product image will show the actual black morph coloration. URL here is the page, not a direct image URL.

summary: The Ocellaris Clownfish (Amphiprion ocellaris) is widely regarded as one of the most popular marine aquarium fish. This entry covers the full species: the orange wild-type, the black Darwin morph, and the many designer morphs sold under distinct trade names. Nearly all specimens available today are captive-bred.
careNotes:
This entry covers Amphiprion ocellaris in its entirety, including the orange wild-type, the black Darwin morph, and all designer morphs listed under this species. Nearly all specimens sold today are captive-bred rather than wild-caught. Darwin-morph juveniles are born orange and gradually transition to black as they mature; designer-morph juveniles vary considerably in early appearance and adult coloration.

Not to be confused with the True Percula Clownfish (Amphiprion percula), which is a SEPARATE species with overlapping but distinct husbandry. The two species are frequently mixed up in the trade, and the Darwin morph of A. ocellaris is often sold under the name 'Black Percula' despite being a different species entirely. Amphiprion percula is documented in its own entry.

Like all clownfish, the Ocellaris is a protandrous hermaphrodite, meaning all individuals are born male and the dominant fish in a group changes sex to become female. When keeping a pair, the best practice is to introduce both fish simultaneously or purchase a confirmed mated pair, as this tends to reduce aggression between them.

The Ocellaris can be territorial around a host anemone and has been known to harass tankmates that approach too closely. It is best kept singly or as a mated pair; introducing multiple individuals at the same time may help reduce aggression among them. Compatible host anemones include Heteractis magnifica, Stichodactyla gigantea, and Stichodactyla mertensii, though an anemone is generally not required for the fish to thrive in captivity.

Wild habitat data places the species' natural temperature range at approximately 26.2–29.3°C, while the aquarium hobby commonly accepts a broader range of 23–29°C to reflect tank tolerance rather than the wild optimum. Wild specimens have been reported surviving salinities as low as 15 ppt without apparent ill effect, though standard reef salinity (specific gravity 1.020–1.025) is generally advised. Reported lifespans in well-maintained aquariums range from around 6 to over 15 years; the wide spread reflects differences in husbandry quality rather than a biological inconsistency.

A number of captive-bred designer morphs are sold under distinct trade names, each representing a different color or pattern variant of the same biological species with identical husbandry requirements. The "Black Ice" morph has a mostly white body with minimal orange. "Black Storm" displays high-contrast black coloration with irregular white striping. "Davinci" is recognized by irregular, swirled white striping. "Gladiator" is an ORA morph characterized by bold, irregular black-and-white patterning overlaid on a standard orange base. "Wyoming White" has a near-uniform white body with dark fins that mature to jet black and a black-outlined orange facial region; it was originally produced by C-Quest and later reintroduced by Sea & Reef Aquaculture. "Flurry," a Sea & Reef cross between Wyoming White and Ultra Snowflake Ocellaris, has a near-pure-white body with only sparse black spots, retains more orange in the fins than the Wyoming White parent, and frequently has pearly-colored eyes. The ORA "Mocha" line includes "Mocha Storm" — a cross between Black Storm and orange Ocellaris that retains the Storm morph's bold irregular white pattern on a chocolate-brown rather than jet-black base — and "Mocha Gladiator," a cross between Black Ocellaris and Wyoming White / Gladiator lines; both deepen in color as the fish matures. Sea & Reef Aquaculture's "Orange Storm," released in December 2018, is the third in their Storm line, created by crossing Black Storm with a bright orange Ocellaris over seven generations to produce the Storm morph's bold irregular white pattern on an orange body rather than the jet-black base. ORA's "Snow Storm" is a cross between a Black Storm male and a Black Snowflake female; it inherits both dominant pattern mutations, producing a crisp white body with primarily black fins edged in icy blue and facial pigment restricted to the lips and eyes.
breedingNotes: Captive-bred specimens have been reported to breed readily in home aquariums. They are egg-layers: females deposit eggs on a flat, clean surface near their host anemone and both parents guard and aerate the clutch. Eggs generally hatch in approximately 6–11 days depending on water temperature. Raising larvae is considered the primary challenge — newly hatched fry require separate rearing tanks and an initial diet of rotifers, transitioning to baby brine shrimp as they grow. Breeding difficulty is generally considered moderate for the spawning phase and more advanced for larval rearing.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (11 cm wild max), temperature range (26.2–29.3°C wild habitat), taxonomy (Pomacentridae, Blenniiformes), and native range. FishBase lists A. ocellaris; confirmed this is the correct species for the Black Percula / Darwin Clownfish trade variant.

additional.name: ORA (Oceans, Reefs & Aquariums)
additional.notes: Confirmed Black Percula Clownfish = Amphiprion ocellaris (Darwin morph), not A. percula. Confirmed near-total captive-bred supply and Darwin, Northern Territory origin. Confirmed juvenile-to-black color transition.

additional.name: Saltwater Aquarium Blog
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22.8–28.8°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (SG ~1.025), adult size (7.6–9 cm), minimum tank (10 gal / 38 L), lifespan (15+ yr). Used to inform parameter averaging.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory (Ocellaris Clownfish care guide)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (23.3–28.3°C / 74–83°F), pH (7.8–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), adult size (7.5–10 cm), minimum tank (10 gal / 38 L), lifespan (avg 6 yr), diet (omnivore). Used to inform parameter averaging.

additional.name: Sea & Reef Aquaculture (designer morph references)
additional.notes: Merge source for multiple designer-morph trade names confirmed as Amphiprion ocellaris. Flurry (Sea & Reef): Wyoming White × Ultra Snowflake Ocellaris cross. Mocha (ORA / Sea & Reef): captive-bred chocolate-brown color line; Mocha Storm = Black Storm × orange Ocellaris (retains Storm white patterning over chocolate-brown base); Mocha Gladiator = Black Ocellaris × Wyoming White / Gladiator. Orange Storm (Sea & Reef, released December 7, 2018): created by crossing Black Storm with a bright orange Ocellaris over seven generations to retain the Storm's bold irregular white pattern on an orange (rather than jet-black) body — third entry in the Storm line. Snow Storm (ORA, https://www.orafarm.com/product/snow-storm-clownfish/): cross between a Black Storm male and a Black Snowflake female; inherits both dominant Snowflake and Storm pattern mutations, producing a crisp white body with primarily black fins edged in icy blue and facial pigment restricted to the lips and black eyes. All listed trade names added to alsoKnownAs and documented in careNotes; husbandry parameters unchanged because all morphs are the same biological species. See also https://www.seaandreef.com/marine-ornamental-fish/clownfish/flurry and https://www.seaandreef.com/marine-ornamental-fish/clownfish/mocha-storm.

fish.breedingNotes: Pairs spawn readily in captivity on flat surfaces near a host anemone; larvae are challenging to raise and require dedicated rearing tanks with rotifers as first food.
